summary.intent: Fix Podman sandbox startup failure caused by sysfs tmpfs overlays that work on Docker but break Podman due to tmpcopyup permission errors under --cap-drop ALL.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented the fix by making hardening_args() backend-aware, skipping sysfs tmpfs overlays for Podman. All 29 tests pass, formatting and clippy are clean, changes committed and PR created.

repo: The sandbox hardening logic lives in crates/tools/src/sandbox/docker.rs and is shared between Docker and Podman backends via the self.cli field
Podman's OCI runtime applies MaskedPaths (including /sys/firmware) by default, providing some masking even without explicit tmpfs overlays
The project uses cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 for formatting and just lint for the full lint pipeline
Tests are run with cargo test -p moltis-tools --lib sandbox::tests::core

code.finding: Sysfs tmpfs overlays for /sys/firmware, /sys/class/dmi, /sys/devices/virtual/dmi, /sys/class/block — now guarded by cli != 'podman'
Call site for hardening_args() — updated to pass self.cli after signature change to hardening_args(is_prebuilt: bool, cli: &str)
New test test_docker_hardening_args_podman verifies sysfs overlays absent for podman while all other hardening flags remain

workflow: Tests, formatting, and clippy were run in parallel where possible to save time
Clippy was run as a background task (TaskOutput pattern) while other work continued

friction: Clippy was run as a background task requiring a task-notification roundtrip to retrieve output, adding a small latency bump
open_items: Config escape hatch deferred: if users report similar tmpcopyup issues with specific Docker configurations, a config option can be added to skip sysfs overlays on Docker too
Sysfs paths /sys/class/dmi, /sys/devices/virtual/dmi, /sys/class/block are unmasked on Podman — accepted as a slightly less hardened but functional tradeoff
